info.title: CoreClaw API
info.description: Canonical English-first OpenAPI contract for the CoreClaw skill repository. This spec focuses on workflow-critical endpoints used by AI agents and reflects verified runtime behavior where it differs from older generated docs.

tags.name: Public
tags.description: Endpoints that can be called without an API key.

tags.name: Runs
tags.description: Run creation, status, history, logs, and abort operations.

tags.name: Results
tags.description: Inline result reading and export operations.

get.description: Search the CoreClaw scraper catalog. Agents should use this endpoint to discover candidate scrapers before calling `/api/scraper` for live schema inspection.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Keyword used to search the scraper store.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of scraper records to return.

scraper.title: Amazon Product By ASIN
scraper.description: Extract product metadata such as seller, brand, pricing, ASIN, and reviews from Amazon product pages.

scraper.title: Amazon Product List By Keywords
scraper.description: Search Amazon by keywords and return structured product listing results.

get.description: Return the live scraper contract. Agents must read this endpoint before constructing `/api/v1/scraper/run`. Use `data.version` exactly as returned, treat `data.parameters.system` as the default platform layer, and derive the shape of `input.parameters.custom` from `data.parameters.custom` rather than hardcoding fields.

properties.name: startURLs
properties.title: Start URLs
properties.description: Seed URLs consumed by the scraper.

custom.description: Live custom parameter descriptor. Inspect this object at runtime and derive the actual request payload from it.

data.readme: Provide one or more start URLs and choose the output fields required by your workflow.

post.description: Start a new Worker run by `scraper_slug`. Verified runtime rule: `callback_url` is optional; include it when webhook orchestration is needed. `version` must come from `/api/scraper`. `input.parameters.custom` is dynamic and must be derived from `/api/scraper`. `input.parameters.system.memory` is measured in MB and corresponds to `memory_bytes` returned by `/api/scraper`.
Launch a saved Task template as a new async run. `callback_url` is required.
Start a new async run using the same parameters as an existing `run_slug`. `callback_url` is required.
Return status and accounting information for a single run.
List historical runs. `status` and `scraper_slug` work as filters when supplied.
Read paginated structured records for a completed or partially completed run. The shape of `data.list[]` is dynamic and depends on the Worker output schema.
Create a downloadable export file for run results. Use this for large datasets or when the caller needs a CSV or JSON artifact instead of inline analysis.
Return a compact log page plus a URL for downloading the full log artifact.

list.content: SYSTEM: Subtask-[28596067108978688] - Preparing the execution environment.

post.description: Abort a running Worker task. Send `run_slug` in the JSON request body.

200.description: Abort returned success with null data.

post.description: Return account balance and remaining traffic quota.

ApiError.description: Generic error envelope returned by CoreClaw.

code.description: Business status code returned by CoreClaw.

data.description: Error payload. Usually `null`, but the server may return any JSON value.

run_slug.description: Unique run identifier returned by CoreClaw.

SystemRunParameters.description: Runtime system parameters used by `/api/v1/scraper/run`. Reuse defaults from `/api/scraper` when possible and override only the values you need.

cpus.description: CPU cores allocated to the Worker.

memory.description: Memory allocation in MB. This corresponds to `memory_bytes` returned by `/api/scraper`.

max_total_traffic.description: Maximum allowed traffic consumption in MB.

proxy_region.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 proxy region code. Defaults to `US` when omitted.

ScraperSystemDefaults.description: Default system parameter values exposed by `/api/scraper`.

memory_bytes.description: Default memory allocation in MB. Map this value to `input.parameters.system.memory` when calling `/api/v1/scraper/run`.

max_total_traffic.description: Default maximum traffic consumption in MB.

CustomParameterDescriptor.description: Opaque live schema descriptor returned by `/api/scraper`. Treat this object as the source of truth for how to build `input.parameters.custom`. Do not assume fixed field names such as `startURLs` unless the live descriptor actually contains them.

custom.description: Scraper-specific input payload. Its structure is dynamic and must be derived from `/api/scraper`.

version.description: Scraper version. Must be copied from `/api/scraper` -> `data.version`.

is_async.description: `true` returns immediately with `run_slug`; `false` performs a synchronous execution flow.

callback_url.description: Webhook URL for async completion callbacks. Optional; include it when webhook orchestration is needed.
Webhook URL for async completion callbacks.
Webhook URL for async completion callbacks.

run_slug.description: Unique run identifier returned by CoreClaw.

err_msg.description: Error message when the run fails; empty otherwise.

scraper_slug.description: Scraper slug associated with the run.

results.description: Number of output records produced so far.

usage.description: Usage charge as a decimal string.

started_at.description: Unix timestamp in seconds when the run started.

finished_at.description: Unix timestamp in seconds when the run finished. `0` means not finished yet.

origin.description: Source that created the run, typically `api` or `web`.

version.description: Scraper version used by the run.

status.description: Optional status filter. `0` means all statuses.

key.description: Field key used in each result record.

format.description: Output format hint such as `text`, `number`, or `date`.

RunResultRecord.description: Single output record. Keys are dynamic and map to `headers[].key`.

run_slug.description: Run slug whose records should be listed.

headers.description: Column definitions for the result records.

run_slug.description: Run slug whose results should be exported.

filter_keys.description: Optional list of field keys to keep in the export. Use `[]` to export all fields.

group.description: Subtask or log stream grouping identifier.

all_logs_url.description: URL for downloading the full log archive.

result_count.description: Number of result records produced by the run.

balance.description: Account balance as a decimal string.

traffic_expiration_at.description: Unix timestamp in seconds when the traffic quota expires.

description.description: Scraper description from the store listing.

InvalidRequestParameters.description: The request body, query string, or field values do not satisfy the current API contract.

Unauthorized.description: The caller is not authorized to access the requested resource.

example.message: Task does not belong to you

ResourceNotFound.description: The requested resource does not exist.

RateLimitExceeded.description: Too many requests were sent in a short period.

example.message: Too many requests, please try again later

UserNotFound.description: The user account associated with the request could not be found.

InvalidApiKey.description: The supplied API key is invalid.

ExpiredApiKey.description: The supplied API key has expired.

BalanceInsufficient.description: The account balance is insufficient for the requested action.

TrafficInsufficient.description: The account does not have enough traffic quota.

example.message: Account traffic is insufficient. Please purchase a traffic package to enable normal operation.

ScraperNotFound.description: The specified scraper does not exist.

ScraperVersionUnavailable.description: The requested scraper version is not available.

example.message: The script version is not available

TaskNotFound.description: The specified task does not exist.

RunRecordNotFound.description: The specified run record does not exist.

AbortRunFailed.description: The platform failed to abort the run.
